mission.title: Install and Configure Pixie on Kubernetes
mission.description: Pixie is an open-source observability tool for Kubernetes applications that provides instant insights into your cluster's performance and resource usage. Installing Pixie allows you to leverage its auto-telemetry and in-cluster edge compute capabilities for enhanced observability.

steps.title: Add the Pixie Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Pixie Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add pixie https://pixie-io.github.io/pixie-helm-charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Pixie using Helm
steps.description:
Install Pixie in a new namespace called 'pixie' with the following command:
```bash
helm install pixie pixie/pixie --namespace pixie --create-namespace --version release/cloud/v0.1.9
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Pixie pods are running successfully with the following command:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace pixie
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Set resource limits for the Pixie deployment to ensure it runs efficiently. Edit the deployment with:
```bash
kubectl edit deployment pixie --namespace pixie
```
Add the following resource limits under the containers section:
```yaml
resources:
  limits:
    cpu: "500m"
    memory: "512Mi"
  requests:
    cpu: "250m"
    memory: "256Mi"
```

steps.title: Access the Pixie UI
steps.description:
Port-forward the Pixie service to access the UI locally:
```bash
kubectl port-forward service/pixie --namespace pixie 8080:80
```
You can now access the Pixie UI at http://localhost:8080.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Pixie will have all pods running in the 'pixie' namespace, and you will be able to access the Pixie UI through port forwarding. Additionally, resource limits will be set for optimal performance.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add pixie https://pixie-io.github.io/pixie-helm-charts
helm repo update
helm install pixie pixie/pixie --namespace pixie --create-namespace --version release/cloud/v0.1.9

resources:
  limits:
    cpu: "500m"
    memory: "512Mi"
  requests:
    cpu: "250m"
    memory: "256Mi"

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher and have Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
